name: VASCULAR MALFORMATION
definition: nidus composed of multiple dysplastic vascular channels connecting feeding arteries with single vascular channel between an artery and a vein area of congenital ectasia of thin-walled small-caliber vessels of the skin (dermis / mucous simple slow-flow malformation + abnormal venous network "cavernous hemangioma" (misnomer)

demographics.Age: present at birth in the early quiescent stage; not usually evident until childhood /
demographics.Prevalence: 2nd most common type of vascular malformation after venous malformation
demographics.Path: infiltrative lesion permeating fat planes + involving multiple tissues consisting of
demographics.Histo: small + large dysplastic postcapillary thin-walled vascular channels with sparse

dominant_finding: decreased marrow signal intensity on T1WI ← intraosseous extension of lesion
discriminating_features: large serpentine flow voids on SE images / high-signal-intensity foci on GRE images =
decreased marrow signal intensity on T1WI ← intraosseous extension of lesion

contrast material rise time of 5-10 seconds

clinical.Cx: bone overgrowth, arterial steal phenomenon, cutaneous ischemia
clinical.Rx: sclerotherapy after lack of evident connection between malformation and deep venous
